title: Background Shorthand & Gradients
description: Learn how to use the CSS background shorthand property, and how to create radial and linear gradients.

tasks.title: Recap
tasks.explainer:
- How to add a background image
- How to control background images repeating
- How to change the position of a background image
- How to change the size of a background image

tasks.title: Background Properties
tasks.explainer:
## Background Properties

So far we have used many `background-*` properties. Here's a summary of them all:

| Property | What it does |
|---|---|
| `background-image` | Sets the background image or gradient |
| `background-position` | Sets where the image is placed |
| `background-size` | Controls the image size |
| `background-repeat` | Controls repeating |
| `background-attachment` | Whether the image scrolls with the page |
| `background-origin` | Where the background starts |
| `background-clip` | How far the background extends |
| `background-color` | Sets a fallback colour |

In this lesson you'll learn how to combine these into one line using the **shorthand** property — and how to create beautiful **gradients**.

tasks.title: Background Shorthand
tasks.explainer:
## Background Shorthand

Instead of writing separate properties, you can combine them all using the `background` shorthand:

```css
div {
  background: url('images/photo.jpg') no-repeat center/cover;
}
```

The order is:

1. Image source — `url('...')`
2. Repeat — `no-repeat`
3. Position — `center`
4. Size — `/cover` (note the `/` before the size value!)

> 🚨 When using shorthand, `background-size` values like `cover` must come **after a `/`** following the position. For example: `center/cover`.

tasks.title: Use the Background Shorthand
tasks.explainer:
## Use the Background Shorthand

Rewrite the `.poster` styles using the `background` shorthand property.

Your shorthand should:
- Use `images/ski.jpg` as the image
- Stop it from repeating
- Position it in the centre
- Use `cover` to fill the container

```css
.poster {
  background: url('images/ski.jpg') no-repeat center/cover;
}
```

Try writing it yourself first!

starterFiles.name: style.css

hints: Replace all four `background-*` lines with one `background:` line.
Remember: size comes after position with a `/` — like `center/cover`.

tasks.title: What are Gradients?
tasks.explainer:
## What are Gradients?

A **gradient** is a smooth transition between two or more colours. CSS lets you use gradients anywhere you'd use an image — including as a background.

There are two main types:

- **Radial gradients** — colours radiate outward from a central point (like a spotlight)
- **Linear gradients** — colours flow in a straight line (like a sunset)

Both are set using the `background-image` property.

tasks.title: Radial Gradients
Add a Radial Gradient

starterFiles.name: style.css

hints: Put the inner colour first and the outer colour second inside the brackets.

tasks.title: Radial Gradient Quiz
tasks.explainer: In a `radial-gradient(red, yellow)`, which colour appears in the **centre**?

options.text: They mix together in the centre

tasks.title: Linear Gradients
Add a Linear Gradient
tasks.explainer:
## Add a Linear Gradient

Add a linear gradient to the `.rectangle` div.

Start with a basic top-to-bottom gradient from **red** to **yellow**:

```css
.rectangle {
  background-image: linear-gradient(red, yellow);
}
```

Then try:
1. Changing the direction with `to right`
2. Using an angle like `45deg`

starterFiles.name: style.css

hints: To change direction, add `to right` as the first argument before your colours.

tasks.title: Linear Gradient Direction Quiz
tasks.explainer: What direction does `linear-gradient(red, yellow)` flow by default?

tasks.title: Gradient Challenge
tasks.explainer:
## Gradient Challenge 🎨

Create a styled banner using both what you've learned today.

Your `.banner` div should:
1. Have a **linear gradient** background using at least two colours of your choice
2. Set the direction to `to right` or use an angle
3. Contain an `<h1>` with some text

Bonus: Try using three colours in your gradient, like:

```css
background-image: linear-gradient(to right, red, orange, yellow);
```

starterFiles.name: index.html
starterFiles.content: Your Title Here

starterFiles.name: style.css

hints: Use `linear-gradient(to right, colour1, colour2)` for a left-to-right gradient.
Pick any CSS colours you like — named colours like `coral`, `teal`, or `purple` all work!
